source_documents: D1884 - Policy for Establishing Criteria for Risk Acceptability

probability_model.description: Platform24 uses a two-factor probability model: P1 (probability that sequence of events leads to hazardous situation) and P2 (probability that hazardous situation results in harm). Combined probability P = P1 x P2.
probability_model.p1_description: Probability that the module/component contributes to a hazardous situation through identified sequence of events
probability_model.p2_description: Probability that exposure to the hazardous situation results in harm

severity_definitions.name: Negligible
severity_definitions.description: Inconvenience or temporary discomfort. No injury or health deterioration.
examples: Minor delay in accessing triage service
Need to restart interview due to technical error
severity_definitions.clinical_interpretation: No clinical intervention required. Patient experience affected but no safety impact.

severity_definitions.name: Minor
severity_definitions.description: Temporary injury or impairment not requiring professional medical intervention.
examples: Minor delay in care that does not affect clinical outcome
Patient anxiety due to unclear information
Unnecessary self-care guidance for condition that would resolve naturally
severity_definitions.clinical_interpretation: May cause patient distress but no medical intervention needed. Condition resolves without treatment.

severity_definitions.name: Serious
severity_definitions.description: Injury or impairment requiring professional medical intervention but fully recoverable.
examples: Patient directed to lower urgency care when higher urgency was appropriate, but treated in time
Delayed treatment of non-urgent condition causing temporary worsening
Incorrect triage leading to unnecessary emergency visit
severity_definitions.clinical_interpretation: Medical intervention required but full recovery expected. May result in extended treatment or hospital stay.

severity_definitions.name: Critical
examples: Urgent condition triaged as non-urgent, leading to delayed critical care
Wrong patient receives care intended for another
Emergency condition not identified by triage
severity_definitions.clinical_interpretation: Risk of permanent impairment, disability, or life-threatening deterioration. Immediate escalation required.

severity_definitions.name: Catastrophic
examples: Life-threatening emergency not identified, patient does not call 112
Systematic triage failure affecting multiple patients
Critical data corruption leading to untraceable patient harm
severity_definitions.clinical_interpretation: Risk of death or permanent life-altering injury. Unacceptable without extraordinary justification.

probability_definitions.name: Incredible
probability_definitions.description: Probability so remote that it can be disregarded. Multiple independent failures required.
probability_definitions.estimation_guidance: Multiple fail-safes in place, all must fail simultaneously. No historical occurrence. Theoretical possibility only.

probability_definitions.name: Remote
probability_definitions.description: Very unlikely to occur during device lifetime. May occur once in many million uses.
probability_definitions.estimation_guidance: No known occurrences in similar systems. Strong mitigations in place with verified effectiveness.

probability_definitions.name: Occasional
probability_definitions.description: May occur several times during device lifetime. Statistically possible in large user base.
probability_definitions.estimation_guidance: May have occurred in similar systems. Some mitigations in place but not fully verified. Moderate complexity in failure path.

probability_definitions.name: Probable
probability_definitions.description: Likely to occur during device operation. Expected to occur regularly.
probability_definitions.estimation_guidance: Has occurred in testing or similar systems. Limited mitigations. Simple failure path with few controls.

probability_definitions.name: Frequent
probability_definitions.description: Expected to occur frequently. High likelihood in normal operation.
probability_definitions.estimation_guidance: Observed in testing. No effective mitigations. Direct path to hazardous situation with minimal controls.

risk_matrix.description: Risk evaluated as Low, Medium, or High based on combined probability and severity. Platform24 uses three-level acceptance: Low (acceptable), Medium (ALARP), High (unacceptable).

low.action_required: Acceptable when risk has been reduced as far as possible and controls are verified as effective. Document rationale.

medium.action_required: Acceptable when justified by evidence that all reasonable mitigations implemented and verified, and further reduction would compromise essential functionality. Document benefit-risk analysis.

high.action_required: Unacceptable. Must be reduced through additional design or process controls. If not feasible, escalate to CTO, CMO, CPO, PRRC for documented benefit-risk justification.

risk_control_priority.description: Risk controls applied in priority order per ISO 14971

priority_order.name: Inherent safety by design
priority_order.description: Architectural or code-level measures that prevent hazards

priority_order.name: Protective measures
priority_order.description: Software or process mechanisms that detect or correct errors

priority_order.name: Information for safety
priority_order.description: Instructions, warnings, and training to inform users of residual risks

evaluation_policy.initial_risk_evaluation: Before any risk controls are applied. Represents worst-case inherent risk.
evaluation_policy.residual_risk_evaluation: After all implemented controls. P1 = initial P1 x reduction factors of all applicable controls.
evaluation_policy.overall_risk_evaluation: Evaluation of total residual risk across all identified hazardous situations, considering cumulative effects. Overall risk acceptable when benefits significantly outweigh residual risks.
evaluation_policy.benefit_risk_analysis_threshold: Required for all Medium risks and mandatory for all High risks that cannot be further reduced.
evaluation_policy.disclosure_requirements: Significant residual risks communicated in device labeling and Instructions for Use. All warnings identified in risk analysis implemented in UI. Contraindications documented.

module_risk_acceptable: Risk reduced to lowest possible level through design or process controls
No further reasonable risk reduction possible without compromising intended functionality
Residual risk rated Low or Medium with documented rationale
module_risk_not_acceptable: Mitigation measures unverified, incomplete, or ineffective
Residual risk introduces new or worsened system-level hazard
overall_risk_acceptable: All identified risks reduced to lowest practicable level
Benefits to patients significantly outweigh residual risks
Device meets all applicable regulatory and safety standards
Significant residual risks clearly communicated in labeling and IFU
Risk management process thoroughly implemented and maintained

contraindications: Patients with life-threatening symptoms, serious conditions or trauma
Patients physically or cognitively unable to use digital technology
Patients with cognitive issues unable to use technology correctly
Patients lacking sufficient language proficiency (conversational variant)
key_warnings: Vid livshotande eller akuta besvär - ring 112
Practitioner's own assessment must be used together with triage result
triage24_specific.safety_class_rationale: Software item that can contribute to hazardous situation but failure unlikely to result in unacceptable risk due to contraindications, warnings, and practitioner oversight
